terms.definition: The ability to find, evaluate, create, and communicate information using digital platforms and devices.
National Education Policy 2020, India's framework outlining coding and digital literacy starting in Class 6.
A technique of typing on a keyboard using all fingers and muscle memory, without looking at the keys.
Words Per Minute; a standardized measurement of typing speed.
The middle row of keys on a computer keyboard (A, S, D, F, J, K, L, ;) where fingers rest.
A visual form of programming where users drag and snap puzzle-like blocks of code together to build programs.
A popular block-based programming language developed by the MIT Media Lab for young learners.
Named containers in computer programming used to store, update, and retrieve data values.
Programming instructions (like 'if-then-else') that perform different actions based on whether a condition is true or false.
Control structures in programming that repeat a specific block of code a set number of times or until a condition is met.
The practice of structured communication and query drafting to get accurate, safe outputs from generative AI systems.
Artificial intelligence systems capable of creating new text, images, code, or media based on prompts.
Practicing safe, responsible, and secure habits online to protect personal information from threats.
Fraudulent communication, usually emails or messages, designed to trick individuals into sharing sensitive credentials.
Small text data files stored on your computer by websites to track preferences, sessions, and behavior.
An extra layer of security requiring two distinct forms of identification before granting account access.
Storing data files online in remote servers, allowing files to be accessed from any connected device.
A collection of cloud-based collaboration, document, spreadsheet, and email tools developed by Google.
An interactive digital grid document containing rows and columns used to organize, calculate, and analyze data.
An expression that calculates the value of a cell. Excel formulas always start with an equal sign (=).
A slide template in presentation software that controls layout formatting, fonts, margins, and themes for all slides.
The speed and rhythm at which a presenter speaks and transitions between slides to keep an audience engaged.
A structural system of organizing digital folders and documents hierarchically in an operating system.
Combinations of keyboard key presses that execute commands quickly (e.g., Ctrl+C for copy).
A web-based graphic design application used to create social layouts, banners, presentations, and documents.
A problem-solving process that involves breaking down complex problems into logical, manageable steps.
A step-by-step sequence of instructions or rules written to solve a problem or perform a task.
The trail of data and online activity left behind by a person using the internet.
Using devices for creation, coding, research, or active skill building rather than passive scrolling or viewing.
Educational Technology; using digital hardware, software, and tools to facilitate learning and instruction.
